title: Create project-manager-lib.sh core library
description:
Create scripts/lib/project-manager-lib.sh with core functions following webapp-profile-manager-lib.sh patterns.

Functions needed:
- get_projects() - Parse ~/.config/jomarchy/projects.conf
- show_projects_list() - Display table with colors
- pick_color() - gum menu for 8 palette colors + custom hex input
- scan_code_directory() - Find git repos in ~/code/
- generate_bash_functions() - Generate project-functions.bash
- generate_hyprland_rules() - Generate project-rules.hypr
- regenerate_all() - Regenerate both + reload hyprland

Config format (pipe-delimited):
KEY|NAME|PATH|PORT|ACTIVE_COLOR|INACTIVE_COLOR

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Library sources cleanly (no syntax errors)
- [ ] get_projects() parses config correctly
- [ ] generate_bash_functions() creates valid bash
- [ ] generate_hyprland_rules() creates valid hyprland conf

title: Test and document project manager
description:
End-to-end testing and documentation for project manager.

Testing:
1. Fresh install flow (no existing projects)
2. Migration flow (import from .bashrc)
3. All CRUD operations
4. Bash launcher functions work (j*, cc*)
5. Hyprland rules apply colors correctly
6. Regeneration after changes

Documentation updates:
- Update CLAUDE.md with project manager section
- Document config format
- Document generated file locations
- Add troubleshooting section

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Fresh install works end-to-end
- [ ] Migration preserves all data
- [ ] CRUD operations work correctly
- [ ] Generated launchers function
- [ ] Hyprland colors apply
- [ ] Documentation complete

title: Build browser-dialog.js - Dialog handling
description: Implement browser-dialog.js tool for alert/confirm/prompt handling. Uses CDP Page.javascriptDialogOpening event. Features: accept/dismiss dialogs, input text in prompts, check dialog status. Priority: MEDIUM - Unblocks tests with alerts/confirms.

title: Build browser-wait.js - Smart waiting capability
description: Implement browser-wait.js tool to eliminate race conditions. Supports waiting for: text content, selectors, URL changes, and custom eval conditions. Uses CDP polling with configurable timeouts. Replaces fixed sleep delays with smart waiting. Priority: HIGH - Eliminates race conditions.

title: Add project-launchers.sh to DEV profile completion message
description:
Update install-jomarchy-dev.sh completion message to mention project launchers.

Add to 'What was installed' section:
  ✓ Project launchers (j*/cc* aliases with colored borders)

Add to 'Next steps':
  • Run 'jhelp' to see available project shortcuts
  • Run 'jomarchy --projects' to customize project colors

Acceptance criteria:
- Completion message mentions project launchers
- Users know about jhelp and jomarchy --projects

title: Add project CRUD operations to library
description:
Add project management functions to project-manager-lib.sh:

Functions:
- add_project() - Interactive: scan ~/code or manual entry, pick color
- edit_project() - Change color, port, path for existing project
- remove_project() - Remove from config (with confirmation)

UX requirements:
- add_project: Offer to scan ~/code first, or manual entry
- edit_project: Show current values, allow changing any field
- remove_project: Require confirmation before deletion
- All operations call regenerate_all() after changes

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Can add project interactively
- [ ] Can edit any project field
- [ ] Can remove project with confirmation
- [ ] Changes persist to projects.conf
- [ ] Regeneration happens automatically after changes

title: Integrate project manager into jomarchy.sh
description:
Update jomarchy.sh to integrate project manager.

Changes needed:

1. Add menu entry in show_management_menu() (around line 73):
   "Manage Projects"

2. Add handler function:
   run_project_manager() {
       if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/project-manager-lib.sh" ]]; then
           source "$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/project-manager-lib.sh"
           project_manager_run
       else
           echo -e "${RED}Error: Project manager not found${NC}"
       fi
   }

3. Add case statement for menu option

4. Add CLI flag --projects (around line 233):
   --projects)
       run_project_manager
       ;;

5. Update help text

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 'Manage Projects' appears in management menu
- [ ] Menu option launches project manager
- [ ] jomarchy --projects works from CLI
- [ ] --help shows new flag

title: Audit and consolidate color definitions across jomarchy
description:
DEFERRED: Large refactoring task (30 files) with low ROI.

Current state:
- 149 duplicate color definitions across 30 install scripts
- common.sh exists as canonical source but most scripts define their own colors
- Works fine, just code duplication

Why defer:
- Risk of introducing bugs across many files
- No functional benefit (colors work)
- Time better spent on features

If addressed later:
1. Create standard header snippet with source + fallback
2. Apply to each script systematically
3. Test each script individually

Original scope: ~30 files need updating

title: Build browser-console.js - Structured console access
description: Implement browser-console.js tool for proper error tracking. Uses CDP Runtime.consoleAPICalled + Log.entryAdded to capture console messages with stack traces, timestamps, and source info. Supports filtering by type (errors/warnings/info). Priority: HIGH - Critical for debugging.

title: Build browser-pages.js - Page/tab management
description: Implement browser-pages.js tool for multi-page testing. Uses CDP Target.getTargets + Target.activateTarget. Features: list all pages, open new pages, switch between pages, close pages, get current page. Priority: MEDIUM - Enables multi-page workflows.

title: Implement .bashrc migration import
description:
Add import_from_bashrc() function to project-manager-lib.sh.

Parse existing PROJECT_CONFIG from ~/.bashrc (lines ~600-776) and convert to new projects.conf format.

Current format to parse:
declare -A PROJECT_CONFIG=(
    [chimaro]="~/code/chimaro|CHIMARO|00d4aa|00a080|3500"
    ...
)

New format:
chimaro|CHIMARO|~/code/chimaro|3500||00d4aa|00a080

Migration steps:
1. Detect if PROJECT_CONFIG exists in .bashrc
2. Parse entries into new format
3. Write to ~/.config/jomarchy/projects.conf
4. Offer to comment out old config in .bashrc
5. Regenerate bash functions and hyprland rules

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Detects existing PROJECT_CONFIG in .bashrc
- [ ] Correctly parses all project entries
- [ ] Converts to new pipe-delimited format
- [ ] Preserves all project data (colors, ports, paths)
- [ ] Offers cleanup of old .bashrc entries

title: Create project-launchers.sh installer
description:
Create scripts/install/project-launchers.sh following existing installer patterns.

Installer actions:
1. Create ~/.config/jomarchy/projects.conf with commented example header
2. Create empty ~/.config/jomarchy/project-functions.bash
3. Create empty ~/.config/jomarchy/project-rules.hypr
4. Add source line to .bashrc (if not present):
   source "$HOME/.config/jomarchy/project-functions.bash"
5. Add source line to hyprland.conf (if not present):
   source = ~/.config/jomarchy/project-rules.hypr
6. Offer to scan ~/code and add projects interactively
7. Generate initial bash functions and hyprland rules
8. Track profile installation with add_installed_profile()

Config file header example:
# Project Configuration for Jomarchy
# Format: KEY|NAME|PATH|PORT|ACTIVE_COLOR|INACTIVE_COLOR
# Example: chimaro|CHIMARO|~/code/chimaro|3500|00d4aa|00a080

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Creates all 3 config files
- [ ] Adds source lines idempotently
- [ ] Offers interactive project setup
- [ ] Generates initial configs
- [ ] Tracks installation in jomarchy profiles

title: Build browser-snapshot.js - Structured page tree
description: Implement browser-snapshot.js tool for LLM-friendly page analysis. Uses CDP DOM.getDocument + Accessibility.getFullAXTree to generate structured text output. Supports text and JSON formats. Priority: VERY HIGH - 1000x token savings (5KB vs 5MB screenshots).

title: Build browser-viewport.js - Proper viewport control
description: Implement browser-viewport.js tool for reliable responsive testing. Uses CDP Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride. Features: set custom width/height, presets (mobile/tablet/desktop), device pixel ratio control. Priority: MEDIUM - Reliable responsive testing.

title: Remove duplicate code between bash-customizations-local.sh and project manager
description:
After integrating project-launchers into the repo discovery flow, the old alias generation code in bash-customizations-local.sh becomes obsolete.

Clean up:
- Remove 'Work Project Aliases' generation (lines 230-285)
- Use project-manager-lib.sh functions instead
- Remove duplicate color codes (use common.sh)
- Update completion message to reference jomarchy --projects

Acceptance criteria:
- No duplicate alias generation code
- Single source of truth for project aliases
- bash-customizations-local.sh is leaner

title: Integrate project-launchers into repo discovery flow
description:
When bash-customizations-local.sh clones repos, automatically add them to projects.conf with:
- Auto-assign colors from palette (cycling through available colors)
- Detect if project has package.json and extract port from scripts.dev
- Generate initial short alias (first letter, avoiding conflicts)
- Run regenerate_all() at the end to create bash functions and Hyprland rules

This replaces the current simple cc* alias generation with the full project manager system.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each cloned repo gets a projects.conf entry
- Colors are assigned automatically from COLOR_PALETTE
- Port is auto-detected from package.json if present
- Short aliases handle collisions (first-come-first-served)
- Hyprland window rules are generated
- User can still customize via jomarchy --projects later

title: Add --regenerate flag to jomarchy command
description:
CLOSED: Won't implement - redundant feature.

The TUI (jomarchy --projects) already regenerates configs automatically after any change. Users shouldn't manually edit projects.conf - they should use the interactive menu.

Original request was for: jomarchy --regenerate

title: new agent session shows status as WORKING but textarea button shows DONE button
description: I dont know why it would ever show DONE I thought we moved to 'COMPLETE' but further it shouldnt show hile status = working only if status = done. Weirder here (see img) are two agents both in working status but different text areas one with done button other with just the voice input

title: Build browser-performance.js - Performance metrics
description: Implement browser-performance.js tool for performance testing. Uses CDP Performance.getMetrics to capture: TTFB, DOM load time, page load time, rendering metrics, JS/CSS coverage. Priority: LOW - Nice to have for performance testing.

title: warmstorm agent starts and immediately shows status as disconnected
description: but i thought we had starting for this state? or maybe it could be connecting

title: Create jomarchy-agent-tools standalone installer
description: Built standalone jomarchy-agent-tools repository with modular installer (5 component scripts), comprehensive 17KB README, project templates, and integration with jomarchy main installer. Follows Mario Zechner's 'prompts are code' philosophy. Provides 24 generic tools with massive token savings (32,425 tokens vs MCP servers).

title: Add Project Launchers to DEV profile menu
description:
Add 'Project Launchers' as a selectable component in install-jomarchy-dev.sh menu.

Add to the gum menu:
  '  Project Launchers (colored borders, j*/cc* aliases)'

When selected, run project-launchers.sh installer which:
- Creates config directories
- Adds source lines to .bashrc and hyprland.conf
- Offers to scan ~/code or import from .bashrc

This makes the project manager a first-class citizen of the DEV profile.

Acceptance criteria:
- Project Launchers appears in DEV component menu
- project-launchers.sh is called when selected
- Works independently of GitHub repo cloning

title: Build browser-network.js - Network request monitoring
description: Implement browser-network.js tool for API testing and debugging. Uses CDP Network.enable + event listeners to track request lifecycle. Captures: URL, method, status, headers, body, timing, response data. Supports filtering (fetch/XHR, failed requests). Priority: HIGH - Essential for API testing.

title: Add interactive menu and entry point
description:
Add project_manager_run() main menu entry point to project-manager-lib.sh.

Menu structure (gum choose):
1. View Projects - show_projects_list()
2. Add Project - add_project()  
3. Edit Project - edit_project()
4. Remove Project - remove_project()
5. Regenerate All - regenerate_all()
6. Import from .bashrc - import_from_bashrc()
7. Exit

Following webapp-profile-manager-lib.sh patterns:
- main_menu() with while loop
- check_dependencies() for gum, jq
- Clean exit handling

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Menu displays all 7 options
- [ ] Each option calls correct function
- [ ] Loop continues until Exit selected
- [ ] Dependencies checked on startup

title: Add gum dependency validation to project manager
description:
Project manager functions assume gum is installed but don't always check.

Add validation at entry points:

- Any functions that might be called directly

Also add helpful error message with install instructions:
'gum is required. Install with: sudo pacman -S gum'

- Graceful exit (not cryptic errors)
